one_time_description: Deferred revenue recorded for unexpended ISC contribution funds, reducing net revenue by $5,439,245 in current year (nil in prior year)
economic_notes: Band operates marina (M'inuw'ilum Marina LP), seafood company (Salish Strait Seafoods Ltd. 20% owned), Spirit Bay Development LP (51% owned), Centre Mountain Joint Venture (industrial site development), forest industry investments through Scia'new Nation Investment Corp. Town centre development underway with contingency that some expenditures may be repayable.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on three grounds: (1) tangible capital assets for roads, bridges, water and utility systems not fully recorded per PSAS; (2) investments in GBEs and limited partnerships could not be verified as financial statements were unavailable or unaudited; (3) town centre development expenditures not fully analyzed and subject to contingency repayment. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. The $5,439,245 deferred revenue for unexpended ISC funds significantly distorts current year revenue vs prior year. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $7,925,878 + CMHC $309,910 + FNHA $444,085 + DFO $924,991 + Govt of Canada $774,919 + BC Gaming $435,412 + Province of BC $93,750 - Deferred Revenue ISC ($5,439,245) = $10,469,700 gross before deferred; OSR includes GBE income, rental, other revenue, interest. Demand loan of $726,497 also outstanding. Five GBE/LP entities with limited financial transparency.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
High ISC dependency (115.86%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
